Nu-Map Docs
Developer Reference
← Back to Nu-Map
nu-map/documentation / src/ / ports/ / ICalendar.js

ICalendar semester structure

Semester types and their weights — the foundation of all span arithmetic. The weight field on each semester type is what makes a 6-month co-op span two slots without any hardcoded table.

Port key
"calendar"
Consumed by
PlannerContext (semGrid, year labels), SemRow/SummerRow (slot rendering), specialTermUtils (span arithmetic)
Override when
Your institution has summer sessions, quarters, trimesters, or a split year format (e.g. "2025–2026")

The weight contract

All span logic in the planner uses one rule: a special term spans into the next semester when termWeight > semSlotWeight. Semester types and special term durations must use the same weight scale.

ℹ
Weight scale: 1.0 = one full academic semester. Half-sessions get 0.5. All semester types in one academic year should sum to a whole number so that year boundaries are exact. NU's four types sum to 3.0.

Contract — top-level fields

FieldTypePurpose
defaultStartYearnumberFirst academic year shown when no saved plan exists.
semesterTypesSemesterType[]Ordered list of all regular term types. Display order within each year follows this array order.
yearAnchorstringLowercase month name that begins the academic year, e.g. "august" or "september". Used to align year labels.
academicYearFormat"single" | "split""single" → "2026"; "split" → "2025–2026". Use "split" if your institution labels years that span two calendar years.

Contract — SemesterType fields

FieldTypePurpose
idstringMachine-readable ID stored in plan state, e.g. "fall", "spring", "sumA".
labelstringDisplay name, e.g. "Fall", "Summer 1".
shortLabelstringAbbreviated label for tight spaces, e.g. "FA", "S1".
weightnumberThe span arithmetic key. 1.0 = one full semester; 0.5 = half-session. Must match the scale used in ISpecialTerms durations.
optionalbooleanIf true, this slot can be collapsed in a condensed view (e.g. summer sessions the student didn't attend).
themestringCSS theme key for row background. Must be one of: "fall", "spring", "summer", "special".
monthsstring[]ISO month strings ("01"–"12") covered by this term. Informational — not used in logic.

Northeastern reference implementation

idlabelweightoptionalthememonths
fallFall1.0falsefall09–12
springSpring1.0falsespring01–05
sumASummer 10.5truesummer05–06
sumBSummer 20.5truesummer07–08

Year total: 1.0 + 1.0 + 0.5 + 0.5 = 3.0. defaultStartYear: 2026, yearAnchor: "august", academicYearFormat: "single".

Generic default

Fall + Spring only, weight 1.0 each. defaultStartYear is new Date().getFullYear() at startup. Covers the majority of semester-system universities globally.

idlabelweightoptional
fallFall1.0false
springSpring1.0false

Common calendar patterns

SystemSemester typesWeightsYear total
Semester (no summer)fall, spring1.0, 1.02.0
Semester + summer (NU-style)fall, spring, sumA, sumB1.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.53.0
Semester + one summer sessionfall, spring, summer1.0, 1.0, 1.03.0
Quarter system (4 quarters)fall, winter, spring, summer0.75, 0.75, 0.75, 0.753.0
Trimester (3 equal terms)term1, term2, term31.0, 1.0, 1.03.0
✦
Keep the year total consistent across your institution (e.g. always 3.0 or always 2.0). The span arithmetic doesn't require a specific total, but a consistent sum makes it easier to reason about multi-year plans.
